description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/ssh/*.pub, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/ssh/*.pub
rationale:
If a public host key file is modified by an unauthorized user, the SSH service
may be compromised.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/ssh/*.pub, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/ssh/*.pub
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rw-r--r--
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/ssh/*_key.pub" file to "0600" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/*_key.pub
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 SSH public host key files must have mode 0644 or less permissive.

warnings.general:
Remediation is not possible at bootable container build time because SSH host
keys are generated post-deployment.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 SSH public host key files must have mode 0644 or less permissive.
stig.vuldiscussion: If a public host key file is modified by an unauthorized user, the SSH service may be compromised.
stig.checktext:
Verify the SSH public host key files have a mode of "0644" or less permissive with the following command:

Note: SSH public key files may be found in other directories on the system depending on the installation.

$ sudo stat -c "%a %n" /etc/ssh/*.pub

644 /etc/ssh/ssh_host_dsa_key.pub
644 /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key.pub
644 /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key.pub
644 /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key.pub

If any key.pub file has a mode more permissive than "0644", this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Change the mode of public host key files under "/etc/ssh" to "0644" with the following command:

$ sudo chmod 0644 /etc/ssh/*key.pub

Restart the SSH daemon for the changes to take effect:

$ sudo systemctl restart sshd.service

title: Verify Permissions on SSH Server Public *.pub Key Files
